title: PlaceBlock terrain occupancy check — skip occupied positions
description:
## Problem
PlaceBlock at Y=66 (wall blocks) fails with blockUpdate timeout because terrain (a hill) already fills the target position. The adapter sends the place packet, the server rejects it since a block already exists, and the blockUpdate event never fires — causing a 5-27 second stall per failed placement.

## Fix Applied
In `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` `case 'place'`:
1. Before attempting placement, check `bot.blockAt(toVec3(x, y, z))`.
2. If the target position already has the EXACT block we're placing → skip (blockPlaced success immediately).
3. If occupied by a DIFFERENT block → log as terrain collision, skip (blockPlaced success — position is physically filled).
4. Log diagnostics: material, position, existing block name.

This prevents the 5-27 second `blockUpdate` timeout stall. A future pre-build terrain clearance phase can mine terrain blocks before placing blueprint blocks.

## Files Changed
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js`: Added occupancy check at start of `case 'place'`

## Priority: P0
